inner_classes.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.out_type
documentation:
Override serialization type. Usually, this designates the return type
of the callable in the `sanitizer` attribute. If this is a two-way type,
it may be a good idea to also use the `parser` attribute to perform
reverse conversion.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.default
documentation:
The default value if the input is None.

Please note that this default is UNCONDITIONALLY applied in class
initializer. It's recommended to at least make an effort to use this
only in customized classes and not in original models.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.default_factory
documentation:
The callable that produces a default value if the value is None.

The warnings in `default` apply here as well.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.db_default
documentation:
The default value used only when persisting the value if it is
`None`.

Only works for primitives. Unlike `default` this can also be set to a
callable that takes no arguments according to SQLAlchemy docs.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.nillable
documentation:
Set this to false to reject null values. Synonyms with
`nullable`. True by default. The default value can be changed by

> setting `AttributesMeta.NULLABLE_DEFAULT`.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.min_occurs
documentation:
Set this to 1 to make this object mandatory. Can be set to any
positive integer. Note that an object can still be null or empty, even
if it's there.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.max_occurs
documentation:
Can be set to any strictly positive integer. Values greater than 1
will imply an iterable of objects as native python type. Can be set to
`decimal.Decimal("inf")` for arbitrary number of arguments.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.schema_tag
documentation:
The tag used to add a primitives as child to a complex type in the
xml schema.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.translations
documentation:
A dict that contains locale codes as keys and translations of field
names to that language as values.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.sub_ns
documentation:
An Xml-specific attribute that specifies which namespace should be
used for field names in classes.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.sub_name
documentation:
This specifies which string should be used as field name when this
type is seriazed under a ComplexModel.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.wsdl_part_name
documentation:
This specifies which string should be used as wsdl message part name when this
type is serialized under a ComplexModel ie."parameters".

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.sqla_column_args
documentation:
A dict that will be passed to SQLAlchemy's `Column` constructor as
`**kwargs`.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.exc_mapper
documentation:
If true, this field will be excluded from the table mapper of the
parent class.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.exc_table
documentation: DEPRECATED !!! Use `exc_db` instead.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.exc_db
documentation:
If `True`, this field will not be persisted to the database. This
attribute only makes sense in a subfield of a `ComplexModel` subclass.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.exc_interface
documentation:
If `True`, this field will be excluded from the interface
document.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.exc
documentation:
If `True`, this field will be excluded from all serialization or
deserialization operations. See `prot_attrs` to make this only apply to
a specific protocol class or instance.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.logged
documentation:
If `False`, this object will be ignored in `log_repr`, mostly used
for logging purposes.

- Primitives can have logger=\`\`'...'\`\` which will

always log the value as `(...)`.

- `AnyDict` can have one of

`('keys', 'keys-full', 'values', 'values-full, 'full')` as logger
value where for `'keys'` and `'values'` the output of `keys()`
and `values()` will be logged up to MAX_DICT_ELEMENT_NUM number of
elements and for `'full'` variants, all of the contents of the dict
will be logged will be logged

- `Array` can also have `logger='full'` where all of the value

will be logged where as for simple `logger=True` only
MAX_ARRAY_ELEMENT_NUM elements will be logged.

- For `ComplexModel` subclasses sent as first value to log_repr,

`logger=False` means a string of form `ClassName(...)` will be
logged.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.sanitizer
documentation:
A callable that takes the associated native type and returns the
parsed value. Only called during serialization.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.parser
documentation:
A callable that takes the associated native type and returns the
parsed value. Only called during deserialization.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.unique
documentation:
If True, this object will be set as unique in the database schema
with default indexing options. If the value is a string, it will be
used as the indexing method to create the unique index. See sqlalchemy
documentation on how to create multi-column unique constraints.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.db_type
documentation:
When not None, it overrides Spyne's own mapping from Spyne types to
SQLAlchemy types. It's a standard SQLAlchemy type marker, e.g.
`sqlalchemy.Integer`.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.table_name
documentation: Database table name.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.xml_choice_group
documentation:
When not None, shares the same <choice> tag with fields with the same
xml_choice_group value.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.index
documentation:
Can be `True`, a string, or a tuple of two strings.

- If True, this object will be set as indexed in the database schema
  with default options.

- If the value is a string, the value will denote the indexing method
  used by the database. Should be one of:

  > ('btree', 'gin', 'gist', 'hash', 'spgist')

  See: <http://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.2/static/indexes-types.html>

- If the value is a tuple of two strings, the first value will denote
  the index name and the second value will denote the indexing method as
  above.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.read_only
documentation:
If True, the attribute won't be initialized from outside values.
Set this to `True` for e.g. read-only properties.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.prot_attrs
documentation:
Customize child attributes for protocols. It's a dict of dicts.
The key is either a ProtocolBase subclass or a ProtocolBase instance.
Instances override classes.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.pa
documentation: Alias for prot_attrs.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.empty_is_none

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.order
documentation:
An integer that's passed to `_type_info.insert()` as first argument
when not None. `.append()` is used otherwise.

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.validate_on_assignment

class_variables.name: spyne.model._base.ModelBase.Attributes.polymap
documentation:
A dict of classes that override polymorphic substitions for classes
given as keys to classes given as values.
The class that holds the constraints for the given type.
These are not the xml schema defaults. The xml schema defaults are
considered in XmlSchema's add() method. the defaults here are to reflect
what people seem to want most.
Please note that min_occurs and max_occurs must be validated in the
ComplexModelBase deserializer.

classes.name: spyne.model._base.xml
documentation:
Compound option object for xml serialization. It's meant to be passed to
{func}`ComplexModelBase.Attributes.store_as`.

```
param root_tag

Root tag of the xml element that contains the field values.

param no_ns

When true, the xml document is stripped from namespace
information. This is generally a stupid thing to do. Use with caution.
```

classes.name: spyne.model._base.table
documentation:
Compound option object for for storing the class instance as in row in a
table in a relational database. It's meant to be passed to
{func}`ComplexModelBase.Attributes.store_as`.

```
param multi

When False, configures a one-to-many relationship where the
child table has a foreign key to the parent. When not False,
configures a many-to-many relationship by creating an intermediate
relation table that has foreign keys to both parent and child classes
and generates a table name automatically. When True, the table name
is generated automatically. Otherwise, it should be a string, as the
value is used as the name of the intermediate table.

param left

Name of the left join column.

param right

Name of the right join column.

param backref

See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/13/orm/relationship_api.html?highlight=lazy#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship.params.backref

param cascade

https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/13/orm/relationship_api.html?highlight=lazy#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship.params.cascade

param lazy

See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/13/orm/relationship_api.html?highlight=lazy#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship.params.lazy

param back_populates

See https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/13/orm/relationship_api.html?highlight=lazy#sqlalchemy.orm.relationship.params.back_populates
```

documentation:
Customizes the given type to be a mandatory one. Has special cases for
{class}`spyne.model.primitive.Unicode` and
{class}`spyne.model.complex.Array`.

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.XmlData
documentation: Items which are marshalled as data of the parent element.

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.XmlAttribute
documentation: Items which are marshalled as attributes of the parent element.

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.XmlAttributeRef
documentation: Reference to an Xml attribute.

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.SelfReference
documentation:
Use this as a placeholder type in classes that contain themselves. See
{func}`spyne.test.model.test_complex.TestComplexModel.test_self_reference`.

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelMeta

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelMeta.__new__
documentation: This function initializes the class and registers attributes.

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelMeta.__prepare__
documentation: We record the order fields are defined into ordered dict, so we can
declare them in the same order in the WSDL.
For Python 3 __prepare__ works out of the box, see PEP 3115.
But we use `Preparable` metaclass for both Python 2 and Python 3 to
support six.add_metaclass decorator

This metaclass sets `_type_info`, `__type_name__` and `__extends__`
which are going to be used for (de)serialization and schema generation.

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.as_dict
documentation:
Represent object as dict.

Null values are omitted from dict representation to support optional
not nullable attributes.

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.get_serialization_instance
documentation:
Returns the native object corresponding to the serialized form passed
in the `value` argument.

```
param value

This argument can be:
* A list or tuple of native types aligned with cls._type_info.
* A dict of native types.
* The native type itself.

If the value type is not a list, tuple or dict, the
value is returned untouched.
```

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.get_deserialization_instance
documentation:
Get an empty native type so that the deserialization logic can set
its attributes.

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.get_flat_type_info
documentation:
Returns a _type_info dict that includes members from all base
classes.

It's called a "flat" dict because it flattens all members from the
inheritance hierarchy into one dict.

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.get_simple_type_info
documentation:
Returns a \_type_info dict that includes members from all base classes
and whose types are only primitives. It will prefix field names in
non-top-level complex objects with field name of its parent.

For example, given hier_delim='.'; the following hierarchy:

```
{'some_object': [{'some_string': ['abc']}]}
```

would be transformed to:

```
{'some_object.some_string': ['abc']}
```

```
param hier_delim

String that will be used as delimiter between field
names. Default is '.'.
```

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.get_simple_type_info_with_prot
documentation: See :func:ComplexModelBase.get_simple_type_info

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.produce
documentation: Lets you create a class programmatically.

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.customize
documentation:
Duplicates cls and overwrites the values in `cls.Attributes` with
`**kwargs` and returns the new class.

Because each class is registered as a variant of the original (\_\_orig\_\_)
class, using this function to generate classes dynamically on-the-fly
could cause memory leaks. You have been warned.

class_variables.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.Attributes.validate_freq
documentation: When `False`, soft validation ignores missing mandatory attributes.

class_variables.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.Attributes.child_attrs
documentation:
Customize child attributes in one go. It's a dict of dicts. This is
ignored unless used via explicit customization.

class_variables.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.Attributes.child_attrs_all
documentation:
Customize all child attributes. It's a dict. This is ignored unless
used via explicit customization. `child_attrs` always take precedence.

class_variables.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.Attributes.declare_order
documentation:
The order fields of the :class:`ComplexModel` are to be declared
in the SOAP WSDL. If this is left as None or explicitly set to
`'random'` declares then the fields appear in whatever order the
Python's hash map implementation seems fit in the WSDL. This randomised
order can change every time the program is run. This is what Spyne \<2.11
did if you didn't set \_type_info as an explicit sequence (e.g. using a
list, odict, etc.). It means that clients who are manually complied or
generated from the WSDL will likely need to be recompiled every time it
changes. The string `name` means the field names are alphabetically
sorted in the WSDL declaration. The string `declared` means in the
order the field type was declared in Python 2, and the order the
field was declared in Python 3.

In order to get declared field order in Python 2, the
{class}`spyne.util.meta.Preparable` class inspects the frame stack in
order to locate the class definition, re-parses it to get declaration
order from the AST and uses that information to order elements.

It's a horrible hack that we tested to work with CPython 2.6 through 3.3
and PyPy. It breaks in Nuitka as Nuitka does away with code objects.
Other platforms were not tested.

It's not recommended to use set this to `'declared'` in Python 2
unless you're sure you fully understand the consequences.

class_variables.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.Attributes.methods
documentation: A dict of member RPC methods (typically marked with @mrpc).

class_variables.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.Attributes.method_config_do
documentation:
When not None, it's a callable that accepts a `@mrpc` method
descriptor and returns a modified version.

class_variables.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.Attributes.not_wrapped
documentation:
When True, serializes to non-wrapped object, overriding the protocol
flag.

class_variables.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModelBase.Attributes.wrapped
documentation:
When True, serializes to a wrapped object, overriding the protocol
flag. When a str/bytes/unicode value, uses that value as key wrapper
object name.
ComplexModel-specific attributes

If you want to make a better class type, this is what you should inherit
from.

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.ComplexModel
documentation:
The general complexType factory. The \_\_call\_\_ method of this class will
return instances, contrary to primivites where the same call will result in
customized duplicates of the original class definition.
Those who'd like to customize the class should use the customize method.
(see :class:`spyne.model.ModelBase`).

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.Array

methods.name: spyne.model.complex.Array.resolve_namespace
documentation: the array belongs to its child's namespace, it doesn't have its own
namespace.

This class generates a ComplexModel child that has one attribute that has
the same name as the serialized class. It's contained in a Python list.

classes.name: spyne.model.complex.Iterable

inner_classes.name: spyne.model.complex.Iterable.Push
documentation:
The push interface to the `Iterable`.

Anything append()'ed to a `Push` instance is serialized and written to
outgoing stream immediately.

When using Twisted, Push callbacks are called from the reactor thread if
the instantiation is done in a reactor thread. Otherwise, callbacks are
called by `deferToThread`. Make sure to avoid relying on thread-local
stuff as `deferToThread` is not guaranteed to restore original thread
context.

This class generates a `ComplexModel` child that has one attribute that
has the same name as the serialized class. It's contained in a Python
iterable. The distinction with the `Array` is made in the protocol
implementation, this is just a marker.

Whenever you return a generator instead of a list, you should use this type
as this suggests the intermediate machinery to NEVER actually try to iterate
over the value. An `Array` could be iterated over for e.g. logging
purposes.
